Special Residential Zoning Laws[NL]Ocean City is under different laws than[NL]Japan. The special residential zoning[NL]laws are an example.
The laws work to provide you with the[NL]necessary living space and confirm your[NL]addresses. The outline of the laws is[NL]as follows:
1) Floor space limited by the number of [NL]household members.[NL]2) Restrictions on living in dangerous[NL]areas such as the power generator block.
3) Registration required for visits longer[NL]than 30 days. Contact the Ocean City[NL]government for details.
Special Corporate Laws[NL]Exemption from customs and the reduction[NL]of income tax and other taxes are applied[NL]more favorably in Ocean City than mainland,
making Ocean City a desirable place for[NL]corporations to relocate. Since there is[NL]a limit to how many corporations can move[NL]here,
these special laws examine candidate[NL]corporations and protect local[NL]corporations.The details of these laws[NL]are as follows:
1) Examination of finances.[NL]2) Examination of profit distribution.[NL]1/3 to half of the savings from reduced[NL]income tax should be re-invested.
3) Newly established offices must give[NL]priority to local employment.[NL]Contact the Ocean City government[NL]for details.
Special National Defense Laws[NL]The JDF is stationed in Ocean City to[NL]defend Ocean City and the entire nation[NL]of Japan.
Residents and corporations are asked to[NL]cooperate with the JDF according to these[NL]special laws, as follows:
1) The JDF may appropriate equipment[NL]belonging to an individual or a[NL]corporation in an emergency.
2) In an emergency, the JDF will assume[NL]law enforcemnt duties.[NL]3) Cooperation is requested if the JDF is[NL]dispatched to the OCU.
The requests will be announced through[NL]the news, the internet, and the emergency[NL]broadcast channel. Contact the Ocean City[NL]government for details.
Located in southern Okinawa, Ocean City[NL]is 5,900m long and 5,500m wide, the[NL]largest man-made island.
Underneath the city lies a power plant that[NL]provides energy and also can move the city.[NL]The island is connected to the main island[NL]via the Ocean City bridge.
Ocean City residential areas consist of[NL]low-rise and mid-rise apartments. Streets[NL]are lined with many trees for people to[NL]enjoy.
Essential services, such as medical[NL]facilities, are well maintained. Rent[NL]control ensures reasonable living costs.
The city hall conference room, located[NL]in the center of Ocean City, is painted[NL]in blue to match the seas surrounding[NL]the city.
Each city council member seat is equipped[NL]with an information terminal for quick[NL]research. Auxiliary equipment includes[NL]a TV relay for live telecasts.
The government buildings are placed in the[NL]center of the island. Among them, the[NL]City Hall is the tallest building in[NL]Ocean City.
It is equipped with the latest[NL]anti-earthquake measures. A monthly[NL]concert by the JDF band is held at the[NL]adjacent community center.
The industrial district is the heart of[NL]Ocean City, providing industrial output[NL]and energy.
As well as production facilities, many[NL]research laboratories developing the[NL]latest technology are located in the[NL]district.
The stock market in the business district[NL]is called the Wall Street of Ocean City.[NL]All transactions are computerized and[NL]open around the clock.
Partially because of the tax exemptions,[NL]there are more than 2,000 companies[NL]listed in this market.
The shopping mall in Ocean City distributes[NL]goods. The entire building is covered with[NL]a transparent ceiling.
International sea travel to Taiwan and[NL]the DHZ departs from the port area. There[NL]are also many souvenir shops for visitors[NL]which sell glassware and other local items.
This district has an active nightlife.[NL]There are rows of tropical bars and exotic[NL]restaurants. It is a safe neighborhood[NL]for women, as well.
Recently, a movie theater opened where you[NL]can view optical films that have been out[NL]of print for 100 years.

The engineering center is managed by the[NL]main corporations of Ocean City: Kirishima[NL]Industries, Taoka Construction, and[NL]Hatada Co.[NEWLINE]They are in charge of maintaining facilities[NL]in Ocean City and running PR activities.

Wharf C10, adjacent to Primary Block[NL](PB) 11, housing Ishizawa Trading, Badacca[NL]Marine Transport, and Andas Enterprises,[NL]is under expansion.[NEWLINE]The expansion will add 30 acres to the[NL]current area.[NEWLINE]Please move all cargo to Wharf B while[NL]Wharf C is shut down for construction.

An elevated highway ("Ocean Route 43^),[NL]which passes over sub-blocks 32 through[NL]to 45 of Primary Block (PB) 4, will be[NL]completed in 2113.[NEWLINE]The opening of Ocean Route 43 will make[NL]your commuting easier.[NEWLINE]We ask the residents to be patient[NL]during construction.

Maintenance work is being done on the[NL]power generator units of Primary Block[NL](PB)13.[NEWLINE]There will be no disruption of power, as[NL]additional power will be supplied from[NL]an adjacent PB.[NEWLINE]Please watch out for large vehicles near[NL]the underground entrance.[NEWLINE]Large replacement parts will be[NL]transported by submarines. Some harbors[NL]may close after midnight for this[NL]construction.

Ocean City is a complex of 13 honeycomb[NL]structures (800m in length) called[NL]Primary Blocks (PB).[NEWLINE]Each PB was constructed in 9 different[NL]areas of Japan and moved its way to[NL]Okinawa using the built-in power unit.[NEWLINE]The harbor and upper structures were[NL]constructed to make Ocean City complete.

The Primary Blocks (PB) of Ocean City were[NL]constructed in 9 areas of Kyushu, Chugoku[NL]and Tokai, since it was more effective[NEWLINE]to build them simultaneously in separate[NL]areas.[NEWLINE]Each PB, measuring over 800m in length, is[NL]larger than a tanker. Construction took 5[NL]years, the self-transportation half a year,[NL]and the facility construction, 3 years.[NEWLINE]The 4 cruise motor units are now used as[NL]power generators.

Ocean City Civilian Facilities[NL]2/3 of Ocean City is used by civilians.[NL]Each block houses an administrative,[NL]corporate, commercial and residential area.[NEWLINE]Each residential area is provided with[NL]schools, hospitals, and public services[NL]to ensure the same standard of living as[NL]the mainland.[NEWLINE]The commercial area offers a shopping mall,[NL]a stadium and an amusement park, and[NL]numerous office buildings.

Ocean City JDF Facilities[NL]The JDF naval base occupies 1/3 of[NL]Ocean City.[NEWLINE]The JDF fleet is stationed here, with[NL]8,000 members of the navy.[NEWLINE]The base itself is small compared to[NL]mainland bases, but is efficiently managed[NL]having a barracks, training facility,[NL]administrative facility and a heliport.

Japan Internal Report: No.046790[NL]Japan has been a leader for the 90 years[NL]since the founding of the OCU,
yet is now falling behind other OCU member[NL]nations in education and the economic[NL]terms. Our disaster prevention agencies[NL]are behind those of other nations.
The Department of Public Safety is[NL]understaffed and our equipment is outdated[NL]to the point where the JDF has better[NL]intelligence than the CIU.
Japan Internal Report: No.046831[NL]The JDF has reported the JDF Yokosuka[NL]Base explosion as an accident. However, the[NL]following evidence shows otherwise.
1) The area of the explosion was far[NL]removed from any fuel or ammo depots.
2) A video of the explosion distributed by[NL]the JDF shows signs of digital retouching.[NL]3) No government authorities have been[NL]allowed to examine the site.
It is still not clear exactly what[NL]transpired there, nor do we have the[NL]equipment to break through their[NL]defenses and find out.
Japan Internal Report: No.046896[NL]JDF General Chief of Staff Masao Sasaki[NL]is well-known within the JDF for his[NL]ambition. His "Theory of Active Defense^[NL]is basically
the same as the USN and Da Han Zhong's[NL]policy of "Military Force as Diplomacy.^[NL]Sasaki is backed by many Parliament[NL]members and could become a minister.
His movements are being closely monitored.[NL]If he takes suspicious actions,[NL]rectification may become necessary.
Japan Internal Report: No.046857[NL]We have received information that JDF[NL]intelligence has infiltrated the police.[NL]Our research has found three suspects.
One female police officer has been dropped[NL]from suspicion following a closer[NL]examination. The other two suspects are[NL]under constant surveillance.
We believe that spies were sent into our[NL]organization to gauge the extent to which[NL]the police and public safety boards know[NL]about the Yokosuka base explosion.
Japan Internal Report: No.047050[NL]We are continuing our investigation into[NL]the JDF emergency response team "MAF^[NL](Mobile Assault Force).
Yesterday, one of our investigators was[NL]involved in a hit-and-run accident. He[NL]escaped with minimal injuries and reports[NL]the accident was staged.
We must view this as a hostile attempt by[NL]JDF forces to deter our investigation.We[NL]have increased precautions since learning[NL]the MAF is directed by Sasaki.
OCU Report: No.1022250[NL]Large-scale activity within OCU forces[NL]since the explosion. The OCU sent[NL]inspectors with a scientific team to[NL]Yokosuka Base.
Following that, several transports were[NL]moved to the OCU Atsugi Base. We believe[NL]there is a secret behind the explosion.
OCU Report: No.1023114[NL]Investigations revealed that the OCU[NL]established secret weapons research labs[NL]in all its member nations.
Our information shows a new wanzer model[NL]was developed. It is considerably larger[NL]than standard wanzers and is clearly in[NL]violation of MULS specifications.
Its armament appears to be double that of[NL]a standard wanzer. A research team leaked[NL]this, and we are awaiting photographs[NL]and schematics.
OCU Report: No.1024339[NL]The CIU has also been investigating OCU[NL]activities. It seems that the size of[NL]their intelligence team has decreased.
However, they are still operating on a[NL]larger scale than our Department of[NL]Public Safety and are equipped[NL]with newer weaponry.
The CIU is currently most active in OCU[NL]nations, especially in Southeast Asia[NL]and the Philippines, possibly due to the[NL]unstable conditions there.
OCU Report: No.1025112[NL]The OCU central government has been[NL]strengthening its friendship treaties[NL]with foreign nations recently.
20 years after the 2nd Huffman War, its[NL]relations with the USN are normalizing,[NL]possibly due to the increasing internal[NL]conflict within the OCU.
However, the OCU military still considers[NL]the USN to be its biggest threat, and USN[NL]naval craft near OCU territorial waters[NL]are closely monitored.
OCU Report: No.1025343[NL]Particle cannons, commonly called beam[NL]weapons, are hard to miniaturize and[NL]require a high-output generator.
Velda Co. of Australia offered to make an[NL]experimental beam weapon for the OCU.[NL]It was granted top-secret test privileges[NL]2 years ago.
Jade Metal Lyman Co. was also given[NL]permission to build a heavy combat vehicle.[NL]It seems that Velda has yet to complete[NL]their prototype, and we have been unable
to obtain more information on Jademetal.[NL]We have unconfirmed reports that Velda is[NL]continuing development in a joint venture[NL]with Kirishima Industries.
USN Report: No.310078[NL]USN naval units are patrolling strategic[NL]points in the Pacific. Recently, these[NL]patrols have come very close to OCU[NL]territory.
It is likely they are gathering[NL]information. This data was obtained from[NL]JDF and CIU intelligence reports.
USN Report: No.310125[NL]Mergers in the USN weapons industry have[NL]become increasingly frequent in[NL]recent months.
Fireball Co., Hoprise Co. and Fleiman Co.[NL]all joined the Diable Avionics Group.[NL]Vince Co. has remained independent, but[NL]USN orders have dropped.
Vince Japan, a division of Vince Co.,[NL]declared bankruptcy in 2107, and it is[NL]only a matter of time before the rest of[NL]the company is bought out by Diable.
USN Report: No.310324[NL]We have reports from our USN informant[NL]that the USN is currently developing an[NL]entirely new weapon.
Unfortunately, all contact with our[NL]informant was lost, and we have no more[NL]information. We have reason to believe[NL]that the weapon is an energy beam device.
However, this is not a new type of weapon,[NL]suggesting that the weapon must possess[NL]other capabilities. We will send another[NL]agent to replace our missing informant.
USN Report: No.310454[NL]We have reports from the JDF that USN[NL]naval submarines have been penetrating[NL]OCU territorial waters.
We have confirmation they come as close[NL]as 40km to Okinawa. However, the JDF has[NL]not reported these incidents to the OCU[NL]military, nor to the Japan Ministry of[NL]Defense.
We are currently investigating this[NL]matter. A fault in JDF communications[NL]is suspected.
USN Report: No.310557[NL]As an OCU member nation, Japan stands[NL]opposed to the USN. However, due to strong[NL]trade relations with the USN in the past,
economic cooperation between our two[NL]nations continued after Japan joined the[NL]OCU. Now forces are at work to renew[NL]ties with the USN.
According to our intelligence, certain[NL]political and military elements are[NL]covertly working to establish military[NL]and economic ties with the USN.
Similar activity has been reported in the[NL]USN as well.
Da Han Zhong Report: No.993591[NL]The USN was joined by Da Han Zhong in[NL]strong opposition to the formation[NL]of the OCU.
DHZ officials saw the OCU as an obstacle[NL]to trade with the USN, which threatened to[NL]join the smaller nations of Southeast Asia[NL]together in a wall around their own nation.
Even today, relations are strained, and[NL]nearly all political overtures on the[NL]part of the OCU have been roundly[NL]dismissed by the DHZ.
This has also made penetration of the DHZ[NL]by our agents extremely difficult, forcing[NL]us to greatly restrict our intelligence[NL]operations in this area.
Da Han Zhong Report: No.993968[NL]In the past, the USN often offered Da Han[NL]Zhong economic assistance. Faced with OCU[NL]opposition, the USN
felt it necessary to join with the DHZ,[NL]the latter becoming increasingly dependent[NL]on USN economic support. However, DHZ[NL]chairman Jie Bo Liao,
who assumed control after the death of Bai[NL]Tai Luong, took a hardline stance against[NL]the growing free DHZ economy and severed[NL]all economic ties with the USN.
Our intelligence shows that the USN made[NL]no formal protest, but they began[NL]supplying wanzers and other military aid[NL]to the Hua Lian anti-government rebels.
Da Han Zhong Report: No.994935[NL]Da Han Zhong agents have been discovered[NL]in Japan. We have confirmation of a vessel[NL]leaving the DHZ Shanghai Base.
A similar craft was sighted close to Japan[NL]in the Ito Straits several days later.[NL]We believe this craft was sent to inspect[NL]the JDF Yokosuka Base explosion site.
This evidence of USN and DHZ interest in[NL]the explosion has further reinforced our[NL]belief that this incident was of great,[NL]possibly military, significance.
Da Han Zhong Report: No.995021[NL]One of our agents stationed in[NL]Da Han Zhong has obtained access to[NL]government data.
It appears that the DHZ is preparing for a[NL]large-scale military effort to eradicate[NL]the Hua Lian rebels.
The DHZ is mobilizing its elite Rapid[NL]Reaction Force in what is surely the most[NL]determined assault on the Hua Lian[NL]rebels to date.
Da Han Zhong Report: No.995503[NL]Our agent in Hong Kong has succeeded in[NL]making contact with the Hua Lian rebels.
They hinted to our agent that they are[NL]currently receiving aid from the USN, and[NL]are planning to invade Shanghai. We do[NL]not intend to interfere with their plan.
As their movements will surely have a[NL]great effect on relations between Japan[NL]and the DHZ, we plan to continue our[NL]surveillance of their organization.

[USN and Japan Cooperative][NL]Tension between the OCU and the USN[NL]stems from a suspected USN arms supply to[NL]the rebels in the Philippines.
According to Vice-Minister Yamazaki,[NL]Japan will set up talks with the USN[NL]to relax some of the tension.
Yamazaki plans to visit the USN early[NL]next week to prepare for the talks.[NL]Yamazaki hopes Japan can be a channel[NL]for OCU nations to express concerns.
[Buried Treasure?][NL]The "Rich Bum's Treasure^ legend is well[NL]known in Tokyo. A source inside a major[NL]construction company revealed that the
company actually found the treasure and[NL]hid it. The company denies the allegation.[NL]Authorities are investigating the matter.
[Yokosuka Base Explosion][NL]The investigation into the cause of the[NL]recent explosion on JDF's Yokosuka base[NL]has begun. JDF investigator Asakura
announced that the explosion occured in[NL]a warehouse. The fire started by the[NL]entrance then spread to emergency fuel[NL]stores, thereby causing an explosion.
The cause could have been electrical[NL]related, but most likely it was arson.[NL]The colonel mentioned it could have been[NL]the work of terrorists.
Reports of international terrorism in[NL]OCU countries are on the rise, and the[NL]authorities cannot monitor these[NL]activities in spite of great effort.
The JPO is concerned these events will[NL]spread to Japan and is considering[NL]legislation as one avenue of dealing[NL]with this.
[Defense Minister Bribery Scandal][NL]The publication "Truth of Rumor^ reported[NL]Kirishima Industries bribing Defense[NL]Minister Tabata.
It was reported that Kirishima Industries[NL]gave Tabata a large cash bribe to[NL]influence his decision in the JDF[NL]wanzer trials next quarter.
The article features photos of Tabata's[NL]private secretary leaving a restaurant[NL]with a Kirishima Industries division[NL]manager and testimony from witnesses.
Tabata and Kirishima held a joint press[NL]conference and denied the allegation.[NL]They also spoke of legal action against[NL]the publication.
However, the Public Prosecutor is showing[NL]strong interest in the article and seems[NL]to be preparing for further investigation.
[Slow Economic Growth Forecasted][NL]The Economic Planning Agency presented[NL]this year's economic forecast, and this[NL]year's growth is expected to be below[NL]last year's.
This report cast a shadow over next[NL]year's national budget, currently[NL]under discussion. The defense budget is[NL]expected to suffer major reductions.
Defense reductions normally face[NL]resistance from defense lobbyists, but[NL]this time the reaction was calm in light[NL]of Tabata's upcoming trial.
Domestic heavy industries stock prices[NL]plunged in reaction to this news.
[Coup d'etat][NL]A splinter faction within the JDF began[NL]a coup d'etat several days ago, and[NL]fighting continues between the
factionists and the JDF.[NL]The faction began as a battalion-sized[NL]group, but by morning many units had[NL]defected to the cause.
The size of the faction has grown to[NL]about 1/3 the size of the JDF, with even[NL]whole fleets and air bases joining the[NL]coup faction.
The JDF is planning to release the name[NL]of the coup leader in the next few days.
Although the JDF is using all its resources[NL]to suppress the coup, it will take some[NL]time for things to return to normal.
[Coup d' etat Follow-up][NL]The battles between the JDF and coup[NL]faction haven't had much effect on urban[NL]areas, but heavy fighting continues in
the countryside. Air force and naval coup[NL]supporters are isolated in bases, and[NL]therefore are locked in a stalemate.[NL]However, battles between ground troops are
becoming fierce with heavy losses on both[NL]sides, especially in the Kyushu area. The[NL]JDF announced they will soon have the coup
supressed. Residents are requested to[NL]follow JDF and the police instructions,[NL]however this request may indicate the coup[NL]faction is gaining the upper hand.
The OCU Congress has requested for[NL]reinforcements, but also sent out a[NL]special envoy to begin arbitration.
[Da Han Zhong Invasion][NL]As DHZ troops invaded Ocean City, the[NL]JDF and the coup faction announced a[NL]temporary cease fire.
Ocean City is currently under DHZ control,[NL]despite the JDF's continued resistance.[NL]A joint JDF/coup fleet is heading for[NL]Ocean City at this time.
DHZ announced the invasion is to capture[NL]terrorists, and not any territorial[NL]ambition. Even so this act is a flagrant[NL]violation of sovereignty.
One can't help but conclude their intent[NL]is territorial, as there have been no[NL]requests for terrorists through[NL]diplomatic channels.
The government has decided to take back[NL]Ocean City and sweep the DHZ from Okinawa.[NL]Hopefully, this will set an example to[NL]discourage further aggression.
[OCU Economic Cooperation Pre-session][NL]While in Australia, Parliamentary[NL]Vice-Minister Kaga met with OCU Congressman[NL]Razami. Although details of the meeting
were not released, government sources[NL]revealed the meeting was a pre-session[NL]before the OCU economic cooperation talks[NL]begin.
The main agenda for the talks will be[NL]financial aid for OCU Southeast Asian[NL]countries currently suffering from[NL]serious inflation. It is likely that Japan
and Australia will be asked to provide[NL]financial aid. It is believed the[NL]pre-session was to decide the amount of[NL]financial aid that will be made available.
Judging from Congressman Razami's good[NL]spirits after the meeting, the negotiations[NL]with Japan must have been quite successful.
[Philippine Rebels][NL]Australian diplomat Foster visits the[NL]Philippines to help end the growing[NL]anti-government rebel activity.
However, shortly after Foster's arrival,[NL]numerous military facilities on Leyte[NL]were attacked simultaneously by the rebels.[NL]There were many casualties.
The attacks began after President Lacan[NL]stated, "I will use all the force[NL]necessary to remove these rebels.^
Lacan also order all troops on standby[NL]and declared a civilian curfew.
The Philippines rebel group is led by[NL]former Vice President Bamarm.
[Singapore's Changi Airport Shutdown?][NL]It was revealed that one of OCU's major[NL]airports, Changi Airport, had its[NL]flight control temporarily shut down.
The shutdown lasted only 30 minutes, but[NL]the total shutdown of control radar and[NL]communications equipment caused much panic.
Takeoffs and landings were suspended[NL]during the shutdown. No accidents[NL]occurred.
The cause is still unknown, but according[NL]to the airport authorities, it's very[NL]unusual for everything to malfunction[NL]at once.
It is suspected the failure was caused[NL]by spenders. Airport authorities are[NL]currently tightening security.
[Da Han Zhong Invasion Failed][NL]The DHZ units that invaded the OCU[NL]Philippines began a sudden withdrawal[NL]several days ago.
There has been no confirmation by the DHZ.[NL]Military sources say the DHZ fleet suffered[NL]a devastating blow.
Skeptics believe such a powerful anti-ship[NL]weapon was used and suggest a computer[NL]system flaw or hacking by spenders as[NL]possible causes.
The Philippines has not released details[NL]of the attack against the DHZ fleet, so[NL]the actual cause remains unclear.
Nonetheless, the OCU welcomes the end of[NL]conflict.
[OCU Economic Cooperative Talks][NL]The OCU economic cooperative talks held in[NL]Alordesh have ended. The main topic was[NL]economic aid for Southeast Asian countries.
The framework to satisfy the OCU nations'[NL]financial needs has been decided upon.[NL]The DHZ invasion was also discussed.[NL]OCU Congressman Charles stated,
"A flaw in the OCU's stucture encouraged[NL]the DHZ to attack. To prevent a repeat of[NL]this incident, we request all OCU nations[NL]send more troops to the OCU military.^
However, the other member nations remained[NL]indifferent to the request. Since it is a[NL]fact that the OCU military is becoming
ineffective, similar proposals will[NL]doubtlessly be made again. Japan will have[NL]to cope with sending more troops to OCU[NL]forces.
[OCU Force Reinforcement][NL]The OCU Congress overwhelmingly passed[NL]a proposal to require all OCU nations to[NL]contribute additional troops to the
OCU forces. However, many OCU nations[NL]are opposed to the proposal. Those[NL]nations are asking for reconsideration[NL]of the proposal.
[Philippines Marine Farms][NL]The OCU Marine Economy Agency announced[NL]they will construct a large scale marine[NL]farm in the Philippines.
The facility will be a stock farm with[NL]conventional deep water pumping,[NL]fish-breeding reefs, and large marine buoys.
If this multi-facility proves successful,[NL]other OCU countries may follow in[NL]constructing similar farms.
[OCU Reaction to DHZ's Japan Invasion][NL]The OCU fleet was attacked and severely[NL]damaged by DHZ forces while offshore[NL]of Negros island in the Philippines.
The attacked fleet was the OCU emergency[NL]relief force, en route to Ocean City in[NL]wake of the DHZ invasion.
Following the attack, the OCU Congress[NL]dispatched a fleet with marines, but the[NL]fleet's arrival will be delayed by this[NL]attack.
A navy spokesman announced the damage[NL]from the attack was not serious, and no[NL]ships were sunk.
The spokesman continued to say the[NL]attack will not in any way prevent the[NL]OCU from defending itself from all[NL]foreign incursions.
[Moscow: Kazuki Kano] The Zaftra Republic[NL]and the Ravnui Republic, both former CIS[NL]nations with a history of conflict, have[NL]closed an economic cooperation treaty.
The treaty was signed at the Zaftra prime[NL]minister's residence. The president of[NL]Zaftra and the ambassador general from[NL]Ravnui attended the signing.
After years of conflict, these 2 nations[NL]have suffered economic collapse.[NL]Their immediate goal is the restructuring[NL]of their economic systems.
Economists from both countries are[NL]welcoming the treaty. The treaty includes[NL]the exchange of technology and should[NL]bolster industrial manufacturing.
The former CIS countries are wedged[NL]between the EC and the OCU. If this treaty[NL]stabilizes the 2 nations' economies, other[NL]former CIS members may consider joining.
[Beijing: Shiro Komichi] The DHZ[NL]announced a campaign to erradicate the[NL]Hua Lian Rebels. The elite Rapid[NL]Reaction Force will likely be used.
In recent years, the DHZ has taken[NL]stronger but futile measures against the[NL]rebels, who have influence in the Huanan[NL]Economic Bloc.
Many Huanan residents object to the[NL]central government's policies, resulting[NL]in large numbers of rebel sympathsizers.
The elite Rapid Reaction Force seems to[NL]be in charge of the operation, though[NL]this has not been confirmed.
It is not known when the operation will[NL]commence, but military resistance is[NL]expected.
[Washington: Rikuo Akasaka][NL]The USN announced that they are ready[NL]to provide industrial technology to the DHZ.[NL]Military technology is not included in[NL]the deal.
The deal is part of the continuing effort[NL]for economic alliance with the DHZ and[NL]includes general industrial products,[NL]automobiles, and aircraft.
This will give the DHZ industrial[NL]capability comparable to the USN or the[NL]OCU.
Some critics say a part of the deal can[NL]be interpreted as providing the DHZ with[NL]wanzer technology.
The starting date of the assistance has[NL]not been made public. It may begin this[NL]year, according to USN sources.
[Singapore: Hachio Daiba][NL]The Pacific Headquarters of the OCU army[NL]announced that a USN aircraft violated[NL]Taiwan's airspace.
The OCU army confirmed the USN aircraft[NL]entered DHZ airspace over the South[NL]China Sea, then dropped to low altitude[NL]over Taiwan, where the radar contact was
lost. There were also unconfirmed[NL]sightings of a parachute-like object[NL]falling from a large aircraft in the[NL]eastern mountains of Xinzhu.
Some believe it is an USN invasion.[NL]Others believe this proves a secret[NL]military coalition between the USN and[NL]the DHZ.
The USN and the DHZ have not commented[NL]on this incident. According to the[NL]anti-war group Broadbeans, who have[NL]close ties to the rebels, there is no[NL]unusual activity.
[Hong Kong: Mitsuo Itsuki][NL]The Hua Lian Rebels have subdued DHZ[NL]government troops as far as Nanjing.[NL]The DHZ is throwing in everything to try and stop
the advance. The battle started in[NL]Wuzhou, spread to Guilin and Foshan, and[NL]is approaching Shanghai.
The DHZ is deploying the Rapid Reaction[NL]Force, as well as standard troops,[NL]without much success. The DHZ forces[NL]appear to have suffered heavy casualties.
There is a rumor that the son of the late[NL]Vice-Chairman Ming Huang Jiu is still[NL]alive and among the rebels, and reports[NL]show foreigners are among the rebels.
It is also rumored the USN is involved.[NL]If the rebels reach Shanghai, it is[NL]possible Chairman Jie could be ousted.
[Beijing: Ken Shiwasu] The Hua Lian[NL]Rebels, who fought their way to Shanghai,[NL]were finally defeated by government[NL]troops.
According to the DHZ military, troops[NL]from Guangzhou as well as marines were[NL]used to defeat and cut off the rebels[NL]forces.
There have been no reports on casualties,[NL]or the extent of property damage, though[NL]civilian casualties are expected to be[NL]high.
Heavy fighting was reported in central[NL]Shanghai, New Shanghai International[NL]Airport, and the Ravnui Embassy.
The military will continue mopping up the[NL]rebels and try to seize the leader, Huang.[NL]More troops will be sent to the rebel HQ[NL]in Guangzhou.
[USN: Hajime Atami] The USN advised DHZ[NL]to cease their invasion of Japan.[NL]Secretary of State Kerwin conveyed the[NL]message directly to the DHZ ambassador.
Anti-war groups gathered in front of the[NL]DHZ embassy in Washington to protest.[NL]Violence was reported against DHZ[NL]residents in SF.

Bangkok Economic Block Established[NL]The establishment of the information[NL]network in the beginning of the 21st[NL]century promoted economic globalization,
which in turn led to a formation of[NL]major economic blocks that hold a larger[NL]share of the global market.
Among them was the Bangkok Economic[NL]Block, established by former ASEAN[NL]members in 2005. It became a backbone of[NL]the current OCU.
The Ship Trinidad Captured[NL]A tanker, Trinidad, cruising through the[NL]Panama Canal, was captured in 2011 by a[NL]US battleship.
Americans stopped the Trinidad for an[NL]inspection, but Australia, who owns the[NL]tanker, saw this incident as
a warning by the US against joining[NL]the Bangkok Economic Block.[NL]Their relationship rapidly deteriorated[NL]after this incident.
Australia and Japan Join Economic Block[NL]The Bangkok Economic Block grew to[NL]become one of the greatest economic[NL]powers by 2015.
Japan could no longer ignore their[NL]presence and moved to join the Bangkok[NL]Economic Block.
Together with Australia, Japan set up a[NL]committee to prepare their entrance into[NL]the Bangkok Economic Block.
Union Renamed[NL]With the addition of Australia and Japan,[NL]the Bangkok Economic Block moved to[NL]expand beyond a simple economic coalition.
They renamed the coalition the[NL]"Oceana Community Union,^ or OCU.
A national flag design was chosen by[NL]contest. 2019 marked the beginning of[NL]the OCU.
Change in Economic Structure[NL]2020 saw drastic change in the economy[NL]of countries joining the OCU.
The change was caused by the establishment[NL]of a unified currency system within[NL]the OCU.
The unification of currency, while[NL]causing minor confusion in the[NL]financial sector, was a significant step[NL]towards the birth of the OCU.
Rise of Anti-OCU Movement[NL]While the OCU was on a steady course[NL]towards establishment, certain countries[NL]were in opposition.
The opposing parties saw the OCU as a[NL]backward step from independence.
The opposition in Southeast Asia was[NL]especially strong with regard to Japan[NL]joining.
OCU Cooperative Military[NL]The OCU recognized the need for a[NL]defensive military to protect the union[NL]of countries.
The first cooperative units were[NL]stationed in Australia, Japan and[NL]Indonesia in 2023.
These units developed into the present[NL]OCU military.
OCU Established[NL]The OCU was finally established in 2026,[NL]after careful preparations,[NL]overcoming various obstacles.
The process took 21 years from the[NL]founding of the Bangkok Economic Block,[NL]ending 7 years after the name[NL]Oceana Community Union was decided[NL]upon.
The first meeting of the OCU Congress[NL]was held in Canberra, Australia.[NL]Representatives of each country[NL]proclaimed their commitment to the union.
Korea Joins OCU[NL]Korea decided to join the OCU in 2028.
Situated next to the economic power of[NL]the DHZ, and seeing its own market[NL]growth slowed by the OCU, Korea[NL]determined to join.
Many other Central Asian countries[NL]joined soon after, forming the current OCU.
